name: Progress MS-33 (94P)
description: Progress resupply mission to the International Space Station.
program_description: The International Space Station programme is tied together by a complex set of legal, political and financial agreements between the sixteen nations involved in the project, governing ownership of the various components, rights to crewing and utilization, and responsibilities for crew rotation and resupply of the International Space Station. It was conceived in 1984 by President Ronald Reagan, dur
rocket_fact: The 'Max-Q' callout during launch refers to the moment of maximum dynamic pressure, where structural stress on the vehicle is highest.
